steps.title: Recognize the right triangle and find the hypotenuse
steps.content:
Triangle $MNP$ has sides $MN = 3$ and $NP = 4$, meeting at vertex $N$.

These are two sides of a classic 3-4-5 right triangle. Since the figure shows $NQ \perp MP$ (a right angle at $Q$), the angle at $N$ is the right angle.

By the Pythagorean theorem: $$MP^2 = MN^2 + NP^2 = 3^2 + 4^2 = 9 + 16 = 25$$ $$MP = 5$$

steps.title: Use the area method to find the altitude NQ
steps.content:
The area of triangle $MNP$ can be computed two different ways:

Method 1: Using the two legs as base and height: $$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 3 \cdot 4 = 6$$
Method 2: Using the hypotenuse $MP$ as base and altitude $NQ$ as the height: $$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot MP \cdot NQ = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 5 \cdot NQ$$
Setting the two areas equal: $$6 = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 5 \cdot NQ$$ $$12 = 5 \cdot NQ$$ $$NQ = \frac{12}{5} = 2.4$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
